ar-jalalayn.name: تفسير الجلالين
ar-jalalayn.author: جلال الدين المحلي؛ جلال الدين السيوطي
ar-jalalayn.description: Ultra-concise one-volume tafsir by al-Mahalli and al-Suyuti � a word-by-word gloss that inserts implied words to make each phrase's meaning explicit. Often just a few words per verse, making it the lightest tafsir to fetch. The standard first tafsir in traditional Islamic education. Choose for quick literal meaning, word-level parsing, or when context budget is tight.

ar-ibn-kathir.name: تفسير ابن كثير
ar-ibn-kathir.author: إسماعيل بن كثير
ar-ibn-kathir.description: The definitive hadith-critical tafsir: Ibn Kathir (a trained muhaddith and student of Ibn Taymiyyah) explains verses through Qur'an, Prophetic hadith, and early reports while rigorously grading narration chains as sahih or da'if. Full Arabic text � substantially richer than the abridged English edition. Choose when you need narration-based exegesis with chain authentication, or to verify the strength of reported interpretations.

ar-qurtubi.name: الجامع لأحكام القرآن
ar-qurtubi.author: محمد بن أحمد القرطبي
ar-qurtubi.description: Encyclopedic tafsir focused on legal rulings (ahkam al-Qur'an) with extended cross-madhhab juristic analysis � presents Hanafi, Maliki, Shafi'i, and Hanbali positions on each legal verse. Also covers abrogation (nasikh/mansukh) and asbab al-nuzul extensively. Choose for fiqh questions, halal/haram rulings, or whenever the legal implications of a verse are in question.

ar-al-wasit.name: التفسير الوسيط
ar-al-wasit.author: محمد سيد طنطاوي
ar-al-wasit.description: Modern comprehensive tafsir (15 volumes) by Muhammad Sayyid Tantawi, Grand Imam of al-Azhar. Groups verses thematically with clear linguistic explanation, asbab al-nuzul, and contemporary social application (al-adabi al-ijtima'i). Choose when seeking a moderate Azhari perspective that connects Quranic meaning to modern life.

ar-baghawi.name: معالم التنزيل
ar-baghawi.author: الحسين بن مسعود البغوي
ar-baghawi.description: Classical narration-based tafsir (Ma��lim al-Tanz�l) that compiles early scholarly interpretations with careful curation � avoids weak narrations and Isra'iliyyat more rigorously than its source (al-Tha'labi). Includes qira'at variants and Arabic linguistic notes. Choose when seeking reliable early consensus interpretations at moderate length, or a cleaner alternative to al-Tabari's encyclopedic scope.

ar-tabari.name: جامع البيان عن تأويل آي القرآن
ar-tabari.author: محمد بن جرير الطبري
ar-tabari.description: The foundational tafsir of the Islamic tradition (d. 310 AH / 923 CE) � the primary source nearly all subsequent mufassirin reference. Systematically compiles ALL known early interpretations, often with full narration chains and differing scholarly views, then resolves disagreements with linguistic, grammatical, and poetic evidence. The most comprehensive classical tafsir available. Choose when you need the full range of early scholarly opinion on a verse, historical-linguistic analysis, or the authoritative baseline for any interpretive question. Note: entries can be very long.

ar-saadi.name: تيسير الكريم الرحمن في تفسير كلام المنان
ar-saadi.author: عبد الرحمن بن ناصر السعدي
ar-saadi.description: Clear, benefits-oriented tafsir that excels at extracting practical guidance (fawa'id) from each passage � asks what a Muslim should take away and apply from every verse. Hanbali tradition, influenced by Ibn Taymiyyah's school. Moderate length with an educational (tarbawi) focus. Choose when seeking actionable spiritual lessons, practical application of Quranic principles, or accessible but insightful commentary.

ar-muyassar.name: التفسير الميسر
ar-muyassar.author: مجمع الملك فهد لطباعة المصحف الشريف
ar-muyassar.description: Committee-produced simplified tafsir officially distributed with the Madinah mushaf (King Fahd Qur'an Printing Complex). Brief, mainstream Sunni interpretations that favor majority scholarly views and avoid disputed positions. Choose for authoritative baseline meaning, quick reference, or when a neutral institutional interpretation is preferred.

en-ibn-kathir.name: Tafsir Ibn Kathir (Abridged)
en-ibn-kathir.author: Isma'il ibn Kathir
en-ibn-kathir.description: Abridged English rendering of Ibn Kathir's hadith-based tafsir, preserving the core method of explaining verses via Qur'an, Prophetic hadith, and early reports in shortened form. Some hadith chains and detailed discussions are omitted. For complete text with full narration grading, use ar-ibn-kathir. Choose for accessible English-language narration-based commentary.

en-maarif-ul-quran.name: Ma'arif al-Qur'an
en-maarif-ul-quran.author: Muhammad Shafi' Usmani
en-maarif-ul-quran.description: English translation of Mufti Muhammad Shafi Usmani's comprehensive 8-volume Urdu commentary, rooted in the Hanafi madhhab and Deobandi scholarly tradition. Features structured 'Ma'arif wa Masa'il' sections extracting practical rulings and contemporary applications from each passage. Synthesizes classical sources including Tabari, Ibn Kathir, and Qurtubi. Translation supervised by Mufti Taqi Usmani. Choose for Hanafi legal perspective, practical rulings in English, or as a comprehensive English alternative to Ibn Kathir with South Asian scholarly orientation.

en-tazkirul-quran.name: Tazkirul Quran
en-tazkirul-quran.author: Wahiduddin Khan
en-tazkirul-quran.description: Modern reflective commentary by Wahiduddin Khan (CPS International) emphasizing personal spiritual development, contemplation (tadabbur), and the Qur'an's relevance to contemporary individual life. Light on classical scholarly apparatus, focused instead on drawing spiritual and practical wisdom for modern readers. Choose for personal reflection, spiritual guidance, or when the user wants contemporary relevance and accessibility over traditional exegetical detail.

ar-nathm-aldurar.name: نظم الدرر في تناسب الآيات والسور
ar-nathm-aldurar.author: برهان الدين إبراهيم بن عمر البقاعي
ar-nathm-aldurar.description: Monumental classical tafsir (22 volumes, d. 885 AH / 1480 CE) focused on tanasub — the thematic and structural harmony between verses and surahs. Identifies a central purpose (maqsad) for each surah and demonstrates how each ayah serves that purpose and connects to its neighbors. The only tafsir in the corpus that opens each surah with an explicit maqsad statement (e.g. 'the purpose of this surah is...'), making it uniquely valuable for 'what is surah X about?' queries. Choose when analyzing WHY verses and surahs are ordered the way they are, discourse structure, or the Qur'an's compositional coherence.
ar-nathm-aldurar.choose_when: User asks about the structural coherence of the Quran, why verses or surahs are in a specific order, thematic connections between adjacent passages (tanasub/munasabah), or the compositional purpose (maqsad) of a surah. Al-Biqai is the only mufassir who states an explicit maqsad for each surah at the opening — uniquely suited for 'what is surah X about?' or 'what is the theme of this surah?'

ar-tahrir-wa-tanwir.name: التحرير والتنوير
ar-tahrir-wa-tanwir.author: محمد الطاهر بن عاشور
ar-tahrir-wa-tanwir.description: Premier 20th-century tafsir for Quranic rhetoric (balagha) and stylistic analysis by the Tunisian Maliki scholar Ibn 'Ashur. Analyzes why the Qur'an chose specific word forms, sentence structures, and rhetorical devices, while grounding interpretation in maqasid al-Shari'ah (objectives of Islamic law). Choose for Arabic stylistic analysis, rhetorical questions ('why does the Qur'an say it THIS way?'), or maqasid-oriented interpretation.
ar-tahrir-wa-tanwir.choose_when: User asks about the overall rhetorical strategy of a passage, why the Qur'an structures an argument or narrative the way it does (discourse-level munasaba), maqasid al-Shari'ah, or modern scholarly exegesis. Ibn Ashur works at the discourse level — passage purpose, communicative intent behind particle and clause choices, how rhetoric serves legislative and ethical objectives. Also strong on comparative stylistic analysis across surahs.

ar-kashaf.name: الكشاف عن حقائق غوامض التنزيل
ar-kashaf.author: أبو القاسم محمود بن عمر الزمخشري
ar-kashaf.description: The classical fountainhead of Quranic rhetorical exegesis (d. 538 AH / 1144 CE). Al-Zamakhshari — a Mu'tazili theologian and master grammarian — performs word-level and clause-level analysis of why the Qur'an chose specific morphological forms, syntactic structures, and rhetorical figures. Uses a distinctive dialectical format (fa-in qulta... qultu: 'if you ask... I answer') to anticipate and resolve linguistic objections. Each surah opens with the richest bibliographic metadata in the corpus: Makki/Madani classification with specific exception ayahs, variant ayah counts, revelation order, and alternative surah names. Choose for grammatical parsing (i'rab), rhetorical devices (balagha) at the word level, figurative vs. literal interpretation, or the Mu'tazili rationalist hermeneutic.
ar-kashaf.choose_when: User asks about rhetorical devices (balagha) at the word or clause level, why a specific morphological form or syntactic structure was chosen over alternatives, grammatical parsing (i'rab) within a tafsir context, Mu'tazili or rationalist readings, figurative vs. literal interpretation of divine attributes, or the Quranic concept of i'jaz (inimitability). Zamakhshari works at the word and clause level — morphological choices, syntactic alternatives, individual figures of speech, with poetic evidence. Also the best source for surah-level bibliographic metadata: Makki/Madani classification with exception ayahs, revelation order, and alternative surah names.

en-pickthall.name: The Meaning of the Glorious Koran
en-pickthall.author: Muhammad Marmaduke Pickthall

en-yusufali.name: The Holy Qur'an: Text, Translation and Commentary
en-yusufali.author: Abdullah Yusuf Ali

en-sahih-international.name: The Qur’an: English Translation of the Meanings
en-sahih-international.author: Sahih International

en-al-hilali-khan.name: Interpretation of the Meanings of The Noble Qur’an
en-al-hilali-khan.author: Muhammad Taqi-ud-Din al-Hilali & Muhammad Muhsin Khan

es-noor-international-center.name: El Noble Corán: Traducción de los significados
es-noor-international-center.author: Centro Noor International

ru-abu-adel.name: Коран: Перевод смыслов с кратким толкованием
ru-abu-adel.author: Абу Адель

fr-hamidullah.name: Le Saint Coran: traduction du sens de ses versets
fr-hamidullah.author: Muhammad Hamidullah

fr-rashid-maash.name: Le Coran: Traduction du sens de ses versets
fr-rashid-maash.author: Rachid Maach

it-hamza-roberto-piccardo.name: Il Sacro Corano (la traduzione dei suoi significati)
it-hamza-roberto-piccardo.author: Hamza Roberto Piccardo

id-king-fahd-quran-complex.name: Al-Qur’an al-Karim dan Terjemahannya (Madinah)
id-king-fahd-quran-complex.author: King Fahd Complex for the Printing of the Holy Qur’an

ja-ryoichi-mita.name: 聖クルアーン
ja-ryoichi-mita.author: 三田 了一（ウマル・ミタ）

ja-saeed-sato.name: 聖クルアーン：日亜対訳注解
ja-saeed-sato.author: サイード佐藤
